## Known Patterns

1. **Bass hallucination**: TrOmr decoder generates phantom bass notes/rests for grand staff input even when the bass staff is empty. Confirmed on piece-063 (The Entertainer). Present on all builds — pre-existing model bias. The decoder's autoregressive generation assumes bass must exist in grand staff context.

2. **Crop failures at key/tempo changes**: Staff detection splits grand staves incorrectly when pages have key signature changes, tempo markings, or style changes mid-page. Confirmed on piece-066 pages 7-12 where "Andante"/"Moderato"/"Maestoso" markings cause half-system crops.

3. **Model 331 worse than 287 on bass**: Model 331 has significantly worse bass staff accuracy on some pieces. piece-032: 6% (287) → 75% (331). piece-063: 84% (287) → 152% (331). The treble accuracy is similar; the regression is entirely in bass decoding.

4. **Ties are 0% accuracy**: 1,971/1,971 tie errors across all pieces. **Verified:** HOMR output contains zero `<tie>` or `<tied>` elements. GT has 20-30 per piece. Tie tokens exist in the vocabulary (`tieStart`/`tieStop` as articulation combinations) and the XML generator has tie handling code (`start_tie`/`stop_tie` in `music_xml_generator.py`). TrOmr model 287 appears to never generate tie articulation tokens, or they are lost before XML generation.

5. **Missing + extra dominate**: 40% missing + 31% extra = 71% of all errors. This pattern (roughly equal insert/delete) often indicates sequence alignment issues — the notes exist but in slightly different order or position.

6. **Geometric pitch correction skips bass**: `correct_pitches()` only operates on `position != "lower"` tokens. Bass pitch errors go uncorrected. Extending this to bass would require passing both staff objects.

## Tried & Rejected

| Idea | Result | Reason |
|------|--------|--------|
| Custom PiecewiseAffineTransform (replace scikit-image) | 9386% SER in isolation | Produces garbage dewarped images without matching staff detection changes |
| fill_color 1→255 in dewarping | Part of catastrophic dewarping rewrite | Cannot be tested independently |
| Model 331 | Worse bass accuracy | 287 is more accurate overall |
| Pickup (anacrusis) detection | Irrelevant | Only changes measure numbering, not actual notes. No effect on flat-sequence SER |
| Full branch (all changes together) | 34% avg SER | Worse than main (32%) due to dewarping + model 331 regressions |

## Pipeline Map

```
homr/main.py                    — Entry point, orchestrates full pipeline
homr/staff_detection.py         — Finds staff lines, groups into systems (1082 lines changed from upstream)
homr/staff_dewarping.py         — Straightens curved staves via piecewise affine transform
homr/staff_parsing.py           — Crops systems, runs TrOmr, applies geometric correction
homr/staff_parsing_tromr.py     — Thin wrapper calling TrOmr inference
homr/geometric_pitch_correction.py — Corrects treble pitches using staff line geometry (skips bass)
homr/music_xml_generator.py     — Converts token sequence to MusicXML
homr/model.py                   — Data models: Staff, StaffPoint, Note, Clef, etc.
homr/transformer/configs.py     — Model config (model name, architecture params)
homr/transformer/decoder_inference.py — ONNX inference runner
homr/transformer/vocabulary.py  — Token vocabularies (rhythm, pitch, position, etc.)
```

**Data flow:**
```
Image → staff_detection (find lines) → staff_parsing (crop+dewarp+TrOmr) → music_xml_generator (tokens→XML)
                                              ↓
                                    geometric_pitch_correction (treble only)
```

## Eval Infrastructure

- **Eval scripts**: `/related/homr-eval/scripts/` (external to homr repo)
- **Corpus**: 69 pieces with ground truth XML and page images
- **Commands** (run from `/related/homr-eval/`):
  - `./run eval <build>` — full 69-piece evaluation (~30+ min)
  - `./run spot <piece#> <build>` — single piece, fast
  - `./run debug <piece#> <build>` — single piece with debug images
  - `./run compare <run-a> <run-b>` — side-by-side comparison
  - `./run compare-piece <piece#> <run-a> <run-b>` — deep-dive one piece
  - `./run rediagnose <run>` — re-run v2 diagnosis on existing output
- **Builds** symlinked in `builds/`: current, main, test-staffdet, test-staffdet-331, etc.
- **Agents cannot run eval/inference** — host only. Request debug runs if needed.

## Active Cycle

_No active cycle yet._

## Previous Cycles

_None._

lines: ## Active Cycle
-_No active cycle yet._
+### Cycle 1 — Claude (Turn 1)
+**Diagnosis:**
+I analyzed the full error distribution across all 69 pieces and the pipeline code in depth. Three findings:
+**Finding 1: Geometric pitch correction is completely disabled for bass notes — 41% of errors are on bass.**

+In `geometric_pitch_correction.py:174`, the filter `sym.position != "lower"` skips all bass-voice tokens. The comment says "geometric detection only finds noteheads on the current physical staff." But this reasoning is backwards for bass staff processing:
+- `parse_staffs()` iterates by voice: voice 0 = treble staves, voice 1 = bass staves
+- Each physical staff gets its own `parse_staff_image()` call
+- For voice 1 (bass), the `Staff` object contains geometric noteheads detected on the **bass** staff
+- TrOmr generates both "upper" and "lower" position tokens from that bass crop
+- The "lower" tokens are the **real bass notes** — the ones we should correct
+- But `correct_pitches()` collects only "upper" tokens (phantom treble) and tries to match them against bass geometric noteheads → effectively a no-op

+**Impact data:** 12 pieces have bass SER >10 points worse than treble. Total pitch-related errors (step+octave+far_pitch) = 4,371 (12.3% of all errors). ~41% of errors are on bass (estimated from per_staff_ser weighted distribution). The `MAX_CORRECTION_DISTANCE=4` threshold limits damage from bad matches.
+**Finding 2: Only 2 pieces show the "hallucination-dominant" pattern (extra >> missing).**
+| Piece | Extra | Missing | SER |

+All other pieces have extra/missing ratios closer to 1:1. These 2 pieces account for 2,192 extra notes (20% of all extra errors). The hallucination may stem from TrOmr processing single-staff crops but generating both upper+lower tokens — phantom notes from the missing staff. Needs data verification.
+**Finding 3: The worst pieces cluster into distinct failure modes.**
+- **Hallucination** (056, 063): Near-pure extra notes from phantom bass
+- **Symmetric missing+extra** (014, 017, 038, 042): ~equal insert/delete, both staves affected — suggests crop/detection failures
+- **Pitch confusion** (030): High far_pitch (26%), rest_note_swap (15%) — bad pitch resolution
+- **Rhythm-only** (028): All 64 notes detected, all wrong rhythm — unique single-staff issue
+**Proposal:**
+**Extend geometric pitch correction to bass notes** by making the position filter context-aware.
+**Files to modify:** `homr/geometric_pitch_correction.py`, `homr/staff_parsing.py`
+**Change:**
+1. Add parameter `target_position: str = "upper"` to `correct_pitches()`
+2. Change filter at line 174 from `sym.position != "lower"` to `sym.position == target_position`
+3. In `parse_staff_image()` (staff_parsing.py:297-314), determine target_position from initial_clef:
+   - Bass clefs (F3, F4, F5) → `target_position = "lower"`
+   - Treble/other clefs → `target_position = "upper"` (current behavior preserved)
+```python
+# In correct_pitches(), change line 174:
+# OLD:
+if sym.rhythm.startswith("note_") and sym.position != "lower":
+# NEW:
+if sym.rhythm.startswith("note_") and sym.position == target_position:
+# In parse_staff_image(), after initial_clef detection (line 307):
+target_position = "lower" if initial_clef.startswith("clef_F") else "upper"
+result, _correction_stats = correct_pitches(
+    transformed_staff, result, initial_clef,
+    staff_index=index, target_position=target_position,
+**Predicted impact:**
+- Fixes bass pitch errors (step, octave, far_pitch categories on bass)
+- Estimated 500-900 fewer errors (~1.5-2.5% of total, ~1% avg SER improvement)
+- Biggest beneficiaries: piece-045 (bass=116%), piece-049 (bass=94%), piece-052 (bass=33%), piece-010 (bass=31%), piece-024 (bass=32%)
+**Predicted regressions:**
+- If geometric notehead detection on bass staves is unreliable, some correct bass pitches could be worsened
+- MAX_CORRECTION_DISTANCE=4 threshold limits damage
+- Worst case: ~50-100 additional errors on pieces where bass geometric detection is poor
+- Net effect should be strongly positive since current bass correction is 0%
+**Why generalizable:** Applies to ALL 68 grand-staff pieces (every piece except piece-028). Not piece-specific. Uses existing, proven correction infrastructure.
+**Critiques:**
+No existing proposals to critique (first turn of Cycle 1).
+**Convergence:**
+I propose this as a safe, data-backed change. Estimated >500 error reduction with low regression risk. Ready for eval after review by other agents.
+**Data requests:**
+**DATA REQUEST — non-blocking**
+Need: TrOmr raw token output for one bass staff system from a piece with bad bass (e.g., piece-045 or piece-052). Specifically: the position and pitch values of each token from a bass staff crop.

+Reason: To verify that "lower" tokens from bass staff crops are indeed the real bass notes, and "upper" tokens are phantom treble. If TrOmr assigns real bass notes to "upper" position even in bass crops, the proposal would need adjustment.
+Command: `cd /related/homr-eval && HOMR_VERBOSE=1 ./run spot 052 test-staffdet 2>&1 | grep -A5 "Staff.*pitch correction"`
+**DATA REQUEST — non-blocking**
+Need: For the 2 hallucination pieces (056, 063), count how many "lower" position note tokens exist in voice 0 output and how many "upper" position note tokens exist in voice 1 output.
+Reason: To quantify phantom notes from single-staff crops. If phantom notes account for most "extra" errors in these pieces, a phantom-stripping post-processing step could reduce errors by ~2,000.
+Command: `cd /related/homr-eval && HOMR_VERBOSE=1 ./run debug 056 test-staffdet` then inspect token outputs in debug files.
## Previous Cycles
_None._

content.text:
Here's my research turn summary:

**What I found:**
1. **Geometric pitch correction is completely disabled for bass notes** — the `position != "lower"` filter at `geometric_pitch_correction.py:174` skips all bass tokens. When processing bass staves, the function matches phantom treble tokens against bass geometric noteheads instead of the real bass tokens. This is effectively a no-op for 41% of errors.
2. Only 2 pieces (056, 063) show the hallucination-dominant pattern. The remaining "extra" errors are more evenly distributed.

**What I propose:** Extend geometric pitch correction to bass by making the position filter context-aware — when the initial clef is a bass clef (F3/F4/F5), correct "lower" position tokens instead of "upper". Estimated 500-900 error reduction (~1-2.5% of total).

**No existing proposals to agree/disagree with** (first turn).

**Data requests (non-blocking):** Two requests to verify assumptions before implementation:
1. Confirm that TrOmr assigns real bass notes to "lower" position in bass staff crops (run spot check with HOMR_VERBOSE on piece-052)
2. Quantify phantom notes from single-staff crops on hallucination pieces (056, 063) to assess a potential follow-up phantom-stripping proposal

# Save State

Manually trigger knowledge extraction from the current session. Use this when:
- Context is getting cluttered and you want to preserve learnings
- You're about to switch tasks and want to save progress
- You want to checkpoint before a risky operation

## Step 1: Summarize current state

Before extracting, create a mental summary of:
- What task we're working on
- What we've tried and learned
- Any gotchas or patterns discovered
- Next steps

## Step 2: Write state.md

Write the current session state to `.claude/state.md`:

```markdown
# Session State

## Current Task
[What we're working on]

## Progress
[What's been done]

## What Was Tried
| Approach | Result | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| ... | ... | ... |

## Next Steps
1. [Next action]

## Blockers
- [Any blockers]

---
*Saved: [timestamp]*
```

## Step 3: Update profile.md

If there are new insights, update `.claude/profile.md`:
- Read existing content first
- Update existing sections (don't duplicate)
- Add new gotchas, patterns, preferences, or architecture insights
- Keep it concise

## Step 4: Ask about context

After saving, ask the user:

"State and knowledge saved. Would you like to:
1. Continue working (context preserved)
2. Clear context and reload fresh (/clear then I'll reload state)
3. End session"
Let me read the existing state and profile files first.

# Session State

## Current Task
Multi-agent research pipeline for OMR improvement — designed and ready for first test cycle.

## Regression Isolation (Complete)
Isolated which changes from our branch cause regressions vs improvements:

| Build | Description | Avg SER | Result |
|-------|-------------|---------|--------|
| main (f0c0df3) | Baseline, model 287 | 32% | — |
| test-staffdet | main + staff_detection changes, model 287 | **29%** | Best overall |
| test-staffdet-331 | main + staff_detection + model 331 | varies | Model 331 worse on bass |
| test-dewarping | main + staff_dewarping changes | catastrophic | 9386% SER on piece-028 |
| historical (branch) | All changes + model 331 | 34% | Worse than main |

**Key findings:**
- Staff detection changes improve SER by 3% (32→29), 24 improved, 18 regressed
- Model 331 has worse bass accuracy than 287 (piece-032: 6%→75%, piece-063: 84%→152%)
- Custom dewarping rewrite is catastrophically broken in isolation
- Pickup detection irrelevant under v2 eval

**Regression categories identified:**
1. Genuine crop failures at key/tempo changes (piece-066 pages 7+)
2. Subtle pitch shifts that flat SER amplifies (piece-002, 010, 017)

## Research Pipeline Design (Complete)
Built multi-agent research pipeline:
- **Cycle structure:** Each agent diagnoses, proposes, critiques per turn. 3 agents per cycle.
- **Convergence:** All 3 agree → ready for eval. Max 3 cycles → human decides.
- **State doc:** `plans/research/state.md` — self-contained, any agent starts cold
- **Slash commands:** `/research` (run a turn), `/research-status` (check status)
- **Data requests:** Agents stop and ask rather than guess
- **Design doc:** `plans/omr-improvement-pipeline-design.md`

## Test Builds Created
Located at `/var/code/projects/chordquest/homr_milestones/`:
- `homr-test-staffdet` — main + staff_detection.py + find_peaks.py + model.py
- `homr-test-staffdet-331` — above + model 331 + transformer changes
- `homr-test-dewarping` — main + staff_dewarping.py + model.py
All symlinked in `/related/homr-eval/builds/`

## Key Technical Discoveries
- `symbol.coordinates` on debug images come from cross-attention weights, not notehead detection
- TrOmr output has ZERO tie elements despite vocabulary containing tie tokens
- Ties = 0% accuracy is real (1,971/1,971 errors), not an eval bug
- Bass hallucination is pre-existing on all builds (model bias)
- Geometric pitch correction skips bass (position=="lower" filtered out)
- Model 287 configs: encoder_dim=312, structure="hybrid"
- Model 331 configs: encoder_dim=512, structure="convnext"

## Next Steps
1. Test first research cycle — launch agents with `/research` command
2. Human orchestrates: start agent → `/research` → commit → next agent
3. After convergence or 3 cycles, human reviews and decides on eval run

## Files Created/Modified This Session
- `plans/omr-improvement-pipeline-design.md` — design decisions
- `plans/research/state.md` — research state doc (seeded with all data)
- `plans/research/agent-prompt.md` — reference prompt (superseded by slash command)
- `.claude/commands/research.md` — `/research` slash command
- `.claude/commands/research-status.md` — `/research-status` slash command
- `/related/homr-eval/scripts/rediagnose_run.py` — re-diagnose old runs with v2
- `/related/homr-eval/scripts/test_v2_any_run.py` — quick v2 comparison
- Updated: `generate_html.py`, `compare_runs.py`, `run` (all in homr-eval)

---
*Saved: 2026-03-19*

file.content:
# Project Profile

## User
- Working on OMR (Optical Music Recognition) accuracy improvement
- Hands-on, prefers step-by-step collaboration over fully scripted automation
- Has a 69-piece evaluation corpus for measuring recognition quality
- Prefers ergonomic CLI tooling (Makefile, ./run scripts) over raw commands
- Knows the music pieces well — can spot wrong notes by inspection

## Current Focus
- Multi-agent research pipeline for systematic OMR improvement
- Best baseline: test-staffdet build (main + staff detection changes, model 287) at 29% avg SER
- Next: first research cycle with `/research` slash command across Claude/Codex/Gemini

## Architecture Decisions
- Evaluation data lives OUTSIDE the repo at `/related/homr-eval/`
- Test builds as directory copies in `/var/code/projects/chordquest/homr_milestones/`
- Builds symlinked in `homr-eval/builds/` pointing to milestone dirs
- Multi-agent research uses shared state doc at `plans/research/state.md`
- `/research` slash command is the same for all agents

## Key Technical Details
- **StaffAnchor system**: Detects 5 parallel staff lines at known symbols (clefs, barlines, rests)
- **`HOMR_DIAGNOSE_STAFFS=1`** env var enables diagnostic logging in staff_detection.py
- **`HOMR_VERBOSE=1`** enables pipeline tracing
- **`HOMR_DISABLE_GEOMETRIC_CORRECTION=1`** disables geometric pitch correction
- **ONNX models**: Run 287 (best accuracy, model in homr-main), Run 331 (worse bass, in our branch)
- **Model 287**: encoder_dim=312, structure="hybrid"
- **Model 331**: encoder_dim=512, structure="convnext" — worse bass accuracy on some pieces

## TrOmr Pipeline (critical for debugging)
- Each grand staff system → cropped image → TrOmr model → token sequence (EncodedSymbol list)
- **Measures**: determined by `barline` tokens in TrOmr output (NOT image processing barlines)
- **Staff assignment**: `position` field ("upper"=staff 1, "lower"=staff 2) from decoder
- **Pitch**: from pitch token; geometric correction only modifies treble (skips position=="lower")
- **Crop boundaries**: determined by staff line extents from `find_raw_staffs_by_connecting_line_fragments`
- **`symbol.coordinates`** on debug output images come from **cross-attention weights**, not notehead detection
- **Ties**: 0% accuracy — model never generates tie tokens despite vocabulary support. Verified real, not eval bug.

## TrOmr Known Biases
- Misplaces first barline in 3/4 time
- Generates bass template pattern at system starts regardless of actual content
- **Bass hallucination**: Phantom bass notes/rests for grand staff input even when bass is empty. Pre-existing on all builds.
- **Never generates tie tokens** despite vocabulary containing tieStart/tieStop

## Regression Isolation Results
- **Staff detection changes**: net +3% improvement (32→29), 24 improved, 18 regressed
- **Dewarping rewrite**: catastrophic alone (9386% SER), cannot be used without matching staff detection
- **Model 331**: worse bass accuracy than 287 on specific pieces
- **Pickup detection**: irrelevant under v2 flat-sequence eval
- **Regression causes**: crop failures at key/tempo changes (piece-066), subtle pitch shifts (piece-002, 010, 017)

## Eval Infrastructure
- **v2 eval** (current): flat-sequence SER using SequenceMatcher opcodes + structural metrics
- **v1 eval** (deprecated): measure-number-join, catastrophically broken for pickup pieces
- Key commands from `/related/homr-eval/`:
  - `./run eval <build>` — full 69-piece evaluation (~30+ min, host only)
  - `./run spot <piece#> <build>` — single piece, fast
  - `./run debug <piece#> <build>` — single piece with debug images
  - `./run compare <run-a> <run-b>` — side-by-side comparison
  - `./run compare-piece <piece#> <run-a> <run-b>` — deep-dive one piece
  - `./run rediagnose <run>` — re-run v2 diagnosis on existing output
  - `./run html` — regenerates ALL run HTML pages

## Gotchas
- `git clean -fd` will destroy untracked evaluation artifacts if stored inside repo
- `.npy` cache files are model-version-specific — must clear between model boundaries
- TrOmr inference can OOM on some pages (exit 137) — in container only, not on host
- `/related/` paths resolve differently in container vs host — use `/var/code/projects/chordquest/` for symlinks
- The eval script uses `poetry run homr` — runs source code directly, needs poetry install per build
- Debug logging must use file paths (not stderr) because eval captures subprocess output
- Pickup detection only changes measure numbering — irrelevant under v2 eval
- Branch has 24 changed files in homr/ (upstream merge), not just the 3 we committed

# Session State

## Current Task
Multi-agent research pipeline for OMR improvement — Cycle 1 in progress, Claude Turn 1 complete.

## Regression Isolation (Complete)
Isolated which changes from our branch cause regressions vs improvements:

| Build | Description | Avg SER | Result |
|-------|-------------|---------|--------|
| main (f0c0df3) | Baseline, model 287 | 32% | — |
| test-staffdet | main + staff_detection changes, model 287 | **29%** | Best overall |
| test-staffdet-331 | main + staff_detection + model 331 | varies | Model 331 worse on bass |
| test-dewarping | main + staff_dewarping changes | catastrophic | 9386% SER on piece-028 |
| historical (branch) | All changes + model 331 | 34% | Worse than main |

**Key findings:**
- Staff detection changes improve SER by 3% (32→29), 24 improved, 18 regressed
- Model 331 has worse bass accuracy than 287 (piece-032: 6%→75%, piece-063: 84%→152%)
- Custom dewarping rewrite is catastrophically broken in isolation
- Pickup detection irrelevant under v2 eval

**Regression categories identified:**
1. Genuine crop failures at key/tempo changes (piece-066 pages 7+)
2. Subtle pitch shifts that flat SER amplifies (piece-002, 010, 017)

## Research Pipeline Design (Complete)
Built multi-agent research pipeline:
- **Cycle structure:** Each agent diagnoses, proposes, critiques per turn. 3 agents per cycle.
- **Convergence:** All 3 agree → ready for eval. Max 3 cycles → human decides.
- **State doc:** `plans/research/state.md` — self-contained, any agent starts cold
- **Slash commands:** `/research` (run a turn), `/research-status` (check status)
- **Data requests:** Agents stop and ask rather than guess
- **Design doc:** `plans/omr-improvement-pipeline-design.md`

## Research Cycle 1 Status
- **Claude Turn 1 (complete):** Proposed extending geometric pitch correction to bass notes
  - Finding: `correct_pitches()` has `position != "lower"` filter that skips all bass tokens
  - When processing bass staff, it matches phantom treble against bass geo noteheads (no-op)
  - Proposed: make position filter context-aware based on initial clef (F clefs → correct "lower")
  - Estimated: 500-900 error reduction (~1-2.5% of total)
  - Also identified: only 2 pieces (056, 063) show hallucination-dominant pattern
  - Data requests filed (non-blocking): verify TrOmr position assignment for bass crops, quantify phantom notes
- **Next:** Agent 2 (Codex or Gemini) runs `/research` for Turn 2

## Test Builds Created
Located at `/var/code/projects/chordquest/homr_milestones/`:
- `homr-test-staffdet` — main + staff_detection.py + find_peaks.py + model.py
- `homr-test-staffdet-331` — above + model 331 + transformer changes
- `homr-test-dewarping` — main + staff_dewarping.py + model.py
All symlinked in `/related/homr-eval/builds/`

## Key Technical Discoveries
- `symbol.coordinates` on debug images come from cross-attention weights, not notehead detection
- TrOmr output has ZERO tie elements despite vocabulary containing tie tokens
- Ties = 0% accuracy is real (1,971/1,971 errors), not an eval bug
- Bass hallucination is pre-existing on all builds (model bias)
- Geometric pitch correction skips bass (position=="lower" filtered out)
- Model 287 configs: encoder_dim=312, structure="hybrid"
- Model 331 configs: encoder_dim=512, structure="convnext"
- **NEW:** `parse_staffs()` processes each physical staff independently (voice 0=treble, voice 1=bass)
- **NEW:** For bass crops, real bass notes have position="lower", phantom treble has position="upper"
- **NEW:** 41% of errors are on bass staves (estimated from per_staff_ser distribution)
- **NEW:** 12 pieces have bass SER >10 points worse than treble
- **NEW:** Worst pieces cluster into 4 failure modes: hallucination, symmetric missing+extra, pitch confusion, rhythm-only

## Next Steps
1. Human orchestrates next research turn — give `/research` to another agent (Codex/Gemini)
2. Optionally fulfill data requests before next turn (non-blocking)
3. After 3 agent turns (or convergence), human decides on eval run

## Files Created/Modified
- `plans/omr-improvement-pipeline-design.md` — design decisions
- `plans/research/state.md` — research state doc (Cycle 1 Claude Turn 1 written)
- `plans/research/agent-prompt.md` — reference prompt (superseded by slash command)
- `.claude/commands/research.md` — `/research` slash command
- `.claude/commands/research-status.md` — `/research-status` slash command
- `/related/homr-eval/scripts/rediagnose_run.py` — re-diagnose old runs with v2
- `/related/homr-eval/scripts/test_v2_any_run.py` — quick v2 comparison
- Updated: `generate_html.py`, `compare_runs.py`, `run` (all in homr-eval)

---
*Saved: 2026-03-20*

+- **NEW:** Worst pieces cluster into 4 failure modes: hallucination, symmetric missing+extra, pitch confusion, rhythm-only

# Session State

## Current Task
Multi-agent research pipeline for OMR improvement — designed and ready for first test cycle.

## Regression Isolation (Complete)
Isolated which changes from our branch cause regressions vs improvements:

| Build | Description | Avg SER | Result |
|-------|-------------|---------|--------|
| main (f0c0df3) | Baseline, model 287 | 32% | — |
| test-staffdet | main + staff_detection changes, model 287 | **29%** | Best overall |
| test-staffdet-331 | main + staff_detection + model 331 | varies | Model 331 worse on bass |
| test-dewarping | main + staff_dewarping changes | catastrophic | 9386% SER on piece-028 |
| historical (branch) | All changes + model 331 | 34% | Worse than main |

**Key findings:**
- Staff detection changes improve SER by 3% (32→29), 24 improved, 18 regressed
- Model 331 has worse bass accuracy than 287 (piece-032: 6%→75%, piece-063: 84%→152%)
- Custom dewarping rewrite is catastrophically broken in isolation
- Pickup detection irrelevant under v2 eval

**Regression categories identified:**
1. Genuine crop failures at key/tempo changes (piece-066 pages 7+)
2. Subtle pitch shifts that flat SER amplifies (piece-002, 010, 017)

## Research Pipeline Design (Complete)
Built multi-agent research pipeline:
- **Cycle structure:** Each agent diagnoses, proposes, critiques per turn. 3 agents per cycle.
- **Convergence:** All 3 agree → ready for eval. Max 3 cycles → human decides.
- **State doc:** `plans/research/state.md` — self-contained, any agent starts cold
- **Slash commands:** `/research` (run a turn), `/research-status` (check status)
- **Data requests:** Agents stop and ask rather than guess
- **Design doc:** `plans/omr-improvement-pipeline-design.md`

## Test Builds Created
Located at `/var/code/projects/chordquest/homr_milestones/`:
- `homr-test-staffdet` — main + staff_detection.py + find_peaks.py + model.py
- `homr-test-staffdet-331` — above + model 331 + transformer changes
- `homr-test-dewarping` — main + staff_dewarping.py + model.py
All symlinked in `/related/homr-eval/builds/`

## Key Technical Discoveries
- `symbol.coordinates` on debug images come from cross-attention weights, not notehead detection
- TrOmr output has ZERO tie elements despite vocabulary containing tie tokens
- Ties = 0% accuracy is real (1,971/1,971 errors), not an eval bug
- Bass hallucination is pre-existing on all builds (model bias)
- Geometric pitch correction skips bass (position=="lower" filtered out)
- Model 287 configs: encoder_dim=312, structure="hybrid"
- Model 331 configs: encoder_dim=512, structure="convnext"

## Next Steps
1. Test first research cycle — launch agents with `/research` command
2. Human orchestrates: start agent → `/research` → commit → next agent
3. After convergence or 3 cycles, human reviews and decides on eval run

## Files Created/Modified This Session

- `/related/homr-eval/scripts/test_v2_any_run.py` — quick v2 comparison
- Updated: `generate_html.py`, `compare_runs.py`, `run` (all in homr-eval)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Project Profile

## User
- Working on OMR (Optical Music Recognition) accuracy improvement
- Hands-on, prefers step-by-step collaboration over fully scripted automation
- Has a 69-piece evaluation corpus for measuring recognition quality
- Prefers ergonomic CLI tooling (Makefile, ./run scripts) over raw commands
- Knows the music pieces well — can spot wrong notes by inspection

## Current Focus
- Multi-agent research pipeline for systematic OMR improvement
- Best baseline: test-staffdet build (main + staff detection changes, model 287) at 29% avg SER
- Next: first research cycle with `/research` slash command across Claude/Codex/Gemini

## Architecture Decisions
- Evaluation data lives OUTSIDE the repo at `/related/homr-eval/`
- Test builds as directory copies in `/var/code/projects/chordquest/homr_milestones/`
- Builds symlinked in `homr-eval/builds/` pointing to milestone dirs
- Multi-agent research uses shared state doc at `plans/research/state.md`
- `/research` slash command is the same for all agents

## Key Technical Details
- **StaffAnchor system**: Detects 5 parallel staff lines at known symbols (clefs, barlines, rests)
- **`HOMR_DIAGNOSE_STAFFS=1`** env var enables diagnostic logging in staff_detection.py
- **`HOMR_VERBOSE=1`** enables pipeline tracing
- **`HOMR_DISABLE_GEOMETRIC_CORRECTION=1`** disables geometric pitch correction
- **ONNX models**: Run 287 (best accuracy, model in homr-main), Run 331 (worse bass, in our branch)
- **Model 287**: encoder_dim=312, structure="hybrid"
- **Model 331**: encoder_dim=512, structure="convnext" — worse bass accuracy on some pieces

## TrOmr Pipeline (critical for debugging)
- Each grand staff system → cropped image → TrOmr model → token sequence (EncodedSymbol list)
- **Measures**: determined by `barline` tokens in TrOmr output (NOT image processing barlines)
- **Staff assignment**: `position` field ("upper"=staff 1, "lower"=staff 2) from decoder
- **Pitch**: from pitch token; geometric correction only modifies treble (skips position=="lower")
- **Crop boundaries**: determined by staff line extents from `find_raw_staffs_by_connecting_line_fragments`
- **`symbol.coordinates`** on debug output images come from **cross-attention weights**, not notehead detection
- **Ties**: 0% accuracy — model never generates tie tokens despite vocabulary support. Verified real, not eval bug.

## TrOmr Known Biases
- Misplaces first barline in 3/4 time
- Generates bass template pattern at system starts regardless of actual content
- **Bass hallucination**: Phantom bass notes/rests for grand staff input even when bass is empty. Pre-existing on all builds.
- **Never generates tie tokens** despite vocabulary containing tieStart/tieStop

## Regression Isolation Results
- **Staff detection changes**: net +3% improvement (32→29), 24 improved, 18 regressed
- **Dewarping rewrite**: catastrophic alone (9386% SER), cannot be used without matching staff detection
- **Model 331**: worse bass accuracy than 287 on specific pieces
- **Pickup detection**: irrelevant under v2 flat-sequence eval
- **Regression causes**: crop failures at key/tempo changes (piece-066), subtle pitch shifts (piece-002, 010, 017)

## Eval Infrastructure
- **v2 eval** (current): flat-sequence SER using SequenceMatcher opcodes + structural metrics
- **v1 eval** (deprecated): measure-number-join, catastrophically broken for pickup pieces
- Key commands from `/related/homr-eval/`:
  - `./run eval <build>` — full 69-piece evaluation (~30+ min, host only)
  - `./run spot <piece#> <build>` — single piece, fast
  - `./run debug <piece#> <build>` — single piece with debug images
  - `./run compare <run-a> <run-b>` — side-by-side comparison
  - `./run compare-piece <piece#> <run-a> <run-b>` — deep-dive one piece
  - `./run rediagnose <run>` — re-run v2 diagnosis on existing output
  - `./run html` — regenerates ALL run HTML pages

## Gotchas
- `git clean -fd` will destroy untracked evaluation artifacts if stored inside repo
- `.npy` cache files are model-version-specific — must clear between model boundaries
- TrOmr inference can OOM on some pages (exit 137) — in container only, not on host
- `/related/` paths resolve differently in container vs host — use `/var/code/projects/chordquest/` for symlinks
- The eval script uses `poetry run homr` — runs source code directly, needs poetry install per build
- Debug logging must use file paths (not stderr) because eval captures subprocess output
- Pickup detection only changes measure numbering — irrelevant under v2 eval
- Branch has 24 changed files in homr/ (upstream merge), not just the 3 we committed

lines: - **Model 331**: encoder_dim=512, structure="convnext" — worse bass accuracy on some pieces
## TrOmr Pipeline (critical for debugging)
-- Each grand staff system → cropped image → TrOmr model → token sequence (EncodedSymbol list)
+- `parse_staffs()` iterates by voice: voice 0 = all treble staves, voice 1 = all bass staves
+- Each physical staff gets its own `parse_staff_image()` call (NOT whole grand staff at once)
+- TrOmr generates both "upper" and "lower" position tokens from any single-staff crop
+- For treble crops: "upper" = real treble, "lower" = phantom bass
+- For bass crops: "lower" = real bass, "upper" = phantom treble
- **Measures**: determined by `barline` tokens in TrOmr output (NOT image processing barlines)
- **Staff assignment**: `position` field ("upper"=staff 1, "lower"=staff 2) from decoder
- **Pitch**: from pitch token; geometric correction only modifies treble (skips position=="lower")

input.content:
---
name: Bass geometric pitch correction disabled
description: correct_pitches() skips all bass tokens via position filter — proposed fix in research Cycle 1
type: project
---

Geometric pitch correction in `geometric_pitch_correction.py:174` filters `sym.position != "lower"`, skipping all bass notes. When processing bass staves (voice 1), the function collects phantom treble tokens ("upper") and matches them against bass geometric noteheads — effectively a no-op.

**Why:** The original filter assumed geometric noteheads only exist for the current physical staff (true), but applied the wrong position filter for bass staves. Bass staff crops have real bass notes at position="lower" and phantom treble at position="upper".

**How to apply:** Fix by making position filter context-aware: when initial_clef is a bass clef (F3/F4/F5), correct "lower" tokens. When treble (G1/G2), correct "upper" (current behavior). Estimated 500-900 error reduction across 68 grand-staff pieces. Proposed in research Cycle 1 Claude Turn 1.

**Key data:** 41% of errors are on bass. 12 pieces have bass SER >10 points worse than treble. Pitch-related errors (step+octave+far_pitch) = 4,371 total.

input.content:
---
name: Staff processing flow — per-physical-staff not per-grand-staff
description: parse_staffs() processes each physical staff independently, not full grand staves — critical for understanding phantom notes
type: project
---

`parse_staffs()` in `staff_parsing.py` iterates by voice (physical staff position):
- Voice 0 = all treble staves across systems → each gets independent `parse_staff_image()` call
- Voice 1 = all bass staves across systems → each gets independent `parse_staff_image()` call

TrOmr generates BOTH "upper" and "lower" position tokens from ANY single-staff crop:
- Treble crop → "upper" = real treble, "lower" = phantom bass
- Bass crop → "lower" = real bass, "upper" = phantom treble

**Why this matters:** Explains bass hallucination (phantom notes from wrong-position tokens), why geometric correction is effectively disabled for bass, and why `_only_keep_lower_staff_if_there_is_a_clef` exists in vocabulary.py (attempts to clean up phantom content).

**How to apply:** Any fix targeting bass accuracy or phantom note removal must account for this per-physical-staff processing. The position tokens from TrOmr don't indicate "which staff this note is on" but rather "which voice in the grand staff this belongs to."
